Contact & Advice

Contacting Ian

Me and my team are here to help. Please use the contact details listed to get in touch.

Due to strict Parliamentary protocol, I can only take up casework on behalf of Liverpool West Derby constituents.

You can check if I am your MP on the Parliament website and by using this link: https://members.parliament.uk/members/commons

 

email: ian.byrne.mp@parliament.uk

phone: 0151 222 2345

write to: Ian Byrne, 545 West Derby Road, L13 8AA

in person:
please contact the office for details of the MP surgeries I hold around the constituency

Vauxhall Law Centre

 

My office and The Vauxhall Law Centre are working together to offer an advice service to all people living in the Liverpool West Derby Constituency.

Contact Vauxhall Law Centre at:
Phone: 0151 360 7777
Email: advice@vauxhalllawcentre.org.uk

Vauxhall Law Centre provides advice on all Benefit and Debt issues and will be able to provide you with advice on a wide range of issues such as:

  • Claiming Personal Independence Payments for people who are sick and people with disabilities

  • Universal Credit Claims, Housing Benefit, Debt and a whole range of further advice

Useful Information

For people living in Liverpool West Derby

Special Educational Needs Pathways

Pathways to support understanding of the law relating to Special Educational Needs. These pathways deal with problems related to assessing special educational needs and obtaining educational, health and social care provision. These pathways are now maintained by the Law Clinic at the University of Liverpool with the support of students who study the law relating to children with Special Educational Needs as part of their degree. They are intended as a support tool.

https://www.sendecisions.org.uk/

General Coronavirus advice and support

For the latest advice and guidance on Coronavirus restrictions, testing advice, Track and Trace support payments, help for carers and help for businesses please visit the Liverpool City Council website using the following link: https://liverpool.gov.uk/communities-and-safety/emergency-planning/coronavirus/

Struggling to make ends meet - including food and fuel

If you need money to buy food, sanitary products, essential items for children, essential clothing or cover fuel costs.

If you need white goods, furniture or help to set up home after suffering an emergency or crisis.

Call the Mayoral Hardship Fund and Liverpool Citizens Support Scheme on free phone 0800 456 1523 (lines are open from 8am to 6pm, Monday to Friday)

Find your nearest foodbank using the following link: https://liverpool.gov.uk/foodbanks

Homeless, at risk of homelessness or in rent arrears

Liverpool City Council Housing Options provides help and advice is you are homeless or threatened with homelessness.

Call free on 0800 731 6844

Their website also provides further information on the following link: https://liverpool.gov.uk/housing/homeless-or-at-risk/

Benefit and Debt issues - including Universal Credit, Housing Benefit, Personal Independence Payments

Ian Byrne MP’s Office and The Vauxhall Law Centre are working together to offer an advice service to all people living in the Liverpool West Derby Constituency.

Contact us at:
Phone: 0151 360 7777
Email: advice@vauxhalllawcentre.org.uk

You’re not alone-don’t delay, get help today.  For details of other organisations go to the Liverpool City Council Website on the following link: https://liverpool.gov.uk/debt

Get help with rent or Council Tax

Liverpool City Council offers advice on their website on the following link: https://liverpool.gov.uk/benefits/housing-benefits/housing-benefit/
Or call free on 0800 028 3697

You may also qualify for a council tax discount or exemption-find out at https://liverpool.gov.uk/council-tax/
Or call free on 0800 028 3686



Help with fuel and energy bills

Liverpool City Council Healthy Homes Team offers advice on their website on the following link: https://liverpool.gov.uk/housing/fuel-poverty-and-energy-efficiency/

Or call free on 0800 0121 754

Free School Meals

Apply for free school meals on the Liverpool City Council website on the following link: https://liverpool.gov.uk/benefits/free-school-meals/
Or call 0800 028 3697

Your family could save over £400 per year and your child’s school can save up to £1,900 additional funding for every eligible child.

Liverpool Early Help Guide

Information for families in need of childcare or support is available on the Liverpool City Council website on the following link: https://ehd.liverpool.gov.uk/kb5/liverpool/fsd/home.page

Training and work

For help to find training or work please visit the Liverpool in Work website on the following link https://liverpoolinwork.co.uk/
or call 0151 233 1933

There is also helpful information on the Liverpool City Council website on the following link: https://liverpool.gov.uk/jobs-and-training/

Libraries

Find your nearest library on the Liverpool City Council website on the following link: https://liverpool.gov.uk/libraries/

Or call:
Dovecot Library on: 0151 228 6446 ext 30
Croxteth Library on: 0151 233 3069
West Derby Library on: 0151 233 3069

Access to digital support

Need to get online?

If you don’t have a computer, you can get free support and get online at several locations across the city.

Call 0800 028 3697

If your query involves Liverpool City Council services such as parking, housing or bin collection, you can contact your local councillors here: http://councillors.liverpool.gov.uk/mgFindMember.aspx

Learn more about the Right To Food UK Commission

You can follow Ian Byrne MP on X (formerly Twitter), Facebook & TikTok & view clips on his YouTube channel:


545 West Derby Road L13 8AA | 0151 222 2345 | ian.byrne.mp@parliament.uk
